event: Machine Project opens at 1200 D N Alvarado, Echo Park; inaugural show is Tom Jennings -- Story Teller (runs through Jan 24 2004)
Mark Allen, Pomona College Associate Professor of Art, founds the storefront
Machine Project incorporated as an educational nonprofit
IRS 501(c)(3) determination granted (EIN 75-3193159)
A Machine Project Field Guide to LACMA: 10-hour, 60+ project takeover with 35 artists; cofunded by LACMA Public Programs and Ralph M. Parsons Endowment
James Irvine Foundation awards $1M Arts Innovation Fund grant to Hammer Museum to create Public Engagement A.I.R.; Machine Project named first A.I.R.; research phase begins
Official Hammer A.I.R. residency begins (through Dec 2010); ~75--80 onsite programs produced
Field Guide to LACMA book published by Machine Project Press (ISBN 978-0-9753140-4-3)
Hammer A.I.R. residency concludes
Staff page snapshot lists Mark Allen as ED and Michele Yu as Operations Manager
Machine Project Hammer Museum Public Engagement Artist in Residence Report published, co-authored with Allison Agsten
Gamble House intervention in Pasadena: secret basement restaurant by Bob Dornberger and vortex/puppet sculpture by Patrick Ballard
Pasadena Art Alliance awards grant for Patrick Ballard, Return to Foreverhouse (2-month programming series at storefront)
Machine Project -- The Platinum Collection (Live by Special Request) opens at Tang Teaching Museum, Skidmore College
Tang Platinum Collection exhibition closes
Machine Project: The Platinum Collection book published by Tang/DelMonico-Prestel (ISBN 978-3-7913-5619-8); 318 pages, edited by Mark Allen and Rachel Seligman, designed by Kimberly Varella
Platinum Collection EXTRAVAGANZA book launch at the storefront
Sorry, Atlantis: Eden's Achin' Organ Seeks Revenge (Asher Hartman play, multi-month run at storefront)
Tongue Tied (final-stretch programming)
Closure announcement; final farewell event scheduled for Jan 13 2018
Final farewell event 2--9pm: print sale of limited-edition silkscreen posters (2--6pm) + wrap party with secret performances (6--9pm)
Form 990 closure-year filing: $115,048 revenue, $211,467 expenses, -$96,419 net; net assets drawn down to $34,666
website remains live as archive; no new programming announced; no Form 990 filings visible in ProPublica's standardized extract
Heavy Manners Library founded by Matthew James-Wilson at the same address (1200 N Alvarado St Unit D); about page names Machine Project as predecessor

Charity Navigator records Machine Project on California's AB 488 delinquent registrations list; donations through CN platform disabled. Machine Project's website carries the closure language: 'After fifteen years of experimentation, delirium, and joy, the project of Machine Project (2003--2018) is now concluded.'
